description: Simplify learn:plan workflow by removing JIT Assessment (142 lines), simplifying Profile Update Check (63→15 lines), removing Layer 3 validation documentation, and simplifying User Confirmation (4→2 options)
approach: Phase 2 focuses on reducing code complexity and improving user experience by removing intrusive JIT Assessment that interrupts user flow, simplifying the Profile Update Check to only trigger on empty profiles, removing unimplemented Layer 3 validation documentation, and streamlining User Confirmation to 2 simple options (Accept/Reject). This builds on Phase 1's utility functions (logger.js, json-parser.js) to maintain code quality while reducing ~283 lines of code. The approach preserves core functionality while making the workflow more user-friendly and maintainable.

tasks.title: Simplify Profile Update Check logic
tasks.description: Simplify the Profile Update Check (lines 158-220, 63 lines) to only trigger when profile is empty (no known_topics). Remove complex 30-day check and tech keyword matching logic. Reduce to 15 lines with simple empty profile detection.

modification_points.change: Replace 63-line complex logic with 15-line simplified version

implementation: Read plan.md lines 158-220 to understand current Profile Update Check logic
Replace complex logic with simplified version from implementation plan Phase 2.2
New logic: Check if profile.known_topics is empty or length === 0
If empty, show AskUserQuestion with 2 options: 'Yes, Update' or 'Skip'
If user selects 'yes', call SlashCommand('/learn:profile update --goal ...')
Remove: daysSinceUpdate calculation, goalRelevanceCheck function, missingTechForGoal logic
Keep: Profile reload after update, error handling for update failures
Add logger.warn for empty profile detection (uses Phase 1 logger.js)

commands: cd C:\Project\Claude-Code-Workflow && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('.claude/commands/learn/plan.md', 'utf8'); const lines = content.split('\n'); const checkSection = lines.slice(157, 220).join('\n'); if (checkSection.includes('daysSinceUpdate') || checkSection.includes('goalRelevanceCheck')) { console.error('Complex logic not removed'); process.exit(1); }"

criteria: Profile Update Check reduced from 63 lines to ~15 lines
Only triggers when profile.known_topics is empty or missing
Removed: 30-day check, tech keyword matching, goalRelevanceCheck function
Simplified AskUserQuestion with 2 options only
Uses logger.warn from Phase 1 for empty profile warning
verification: Test with empty profile - should prompt for update
Test with populated profile - should skip check
Verify no references to daysSinceUpdate or missingTechForGoal
Check line count reduction in git diff

tasks.title: Simplify User Confirmation to 2 options
tasks.description: Simplify User Confirmation from 4 options (accept/review/modify/save) to 2 options (accept/reject). Remove complex feedback collection and regeneration logic. Reduce from ~54 lines to ~15 lines.
implementation: Read plan.md lines 764-817 to understand current User Confirmation logic
Replace with simplified 2-option version from implementation plan Phase 2.4
New options: 'Accept' (start learning) and 'Reject' (regenerate plan)
Remove: 'review' option (users can view plan.json manually), 'save' option (plan auto-saved), 'modify' feedback collection
If user selects 'reject', add logger.info and return to Phase 3 for regeneration
If user selects 'accept', show success message with /learn:execute instruction
Use logger.info from Phase 1 to log user decision
Simplify switch statement to if-else for 2 options

commands: cd C:\Project\Claude-Code-Workflow && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('.claude/commands/learn/plan.md', 'utf8'); const confirmSection = content.match(/PLAN_CONFIRMATION_KEY[\s\S]{0,500}options:/); if (confirmSection && confirmSection[0].includes('review') || confirmSection[0].includes('modify')) { console.error('Old options not removed'); process.exit(1); }"

criteria: User Confirmation reduced from 4 options to 2 (accept/reject)
Removed: review, modify, save options and associated logic
Code reduced from ~54 lines to ~15 lines
Uses logger.info from Phase 1 for decision logging
Clear user messaging for both accept and reject paths
verification: Test accept path - should show success message
Test reject path - should trigger plan regeneration
Verify no references to 'review', 'modify', 'save' options
Check line count reduction in git diff
